results.name: Venezuela
results.key_finding: Twin Mw 7.2+7.5 earthquakes (Jun 24; 1,430+ killed, 68,900+ missing) are a natural disaster (exogenous, not own-conduct). The government RESPONSE is mixed own-conduct: broad international aid ACCEPTED (17 flights/1,600+ rescuers, US/Cuba/Mexico/Colombia/Brazil/UN; cuts against downgrade and differentiates from floor-cluster aid-blockers) vs slow, disjointed, opaque relief with heavy access restrictions and performative officials (negative). Nets sub-threshold both directions. Confirm 18.0; watch flags ACT + ACC. Stale 4.4 cache refreshed to authoritative 18.0.

results.name: Nigeria
results.key_finding: 35M IPC Phase 3+ / 15,000 Phase 5 Borno already priced in the Jun 19 band crossing to 18.0. Double-count screened (§3e-bis(4)). WFP imminent-cuts is a forward trigger. No new development Jun 27-28.

results.name: Lebanon
results.key_finding: Israel resumed strikes Jun 28; framework effectively dead (rejected by Hezbollah Jun 27). The forward-trigger positive (implementation) did not materialize — no upgrade. Renewed war harm is Israel's conduct (attribution). No new Lebanese own-conduct either direction.

results.name: Sudan
results.key_finding: Floor reinforced. SAF Jun 28 counterattack (reopened Dalang-Kadugli road) is an armed-operations move; RSF's acceptance of a Quad humanitarian-ceasefire proposal is unimplemented (SAF skeptical) — forward trigger, not realized relief. Famine in El-Fasher/Kadugli; El Obeid siege ongoing. No lower score exists.

results.name: Anthropic
results.key_finding: Mythos 5 partial unblock (Lutnick letter Jun 26, ~100 US orgs; corrects Jun 27 scan) is US Commerce action ON Anthropic (attributed to US, not Anthropic). A partial reversal of a government restriction is not Anthropic's own compassion conduct. Fable 5 stays suspended. Canonical 59.1; boundary watch 0.9pt below Established.

results.name: Apple
results.key_finding: 54-member Congressional Labor Caucus call for an NLRB investigation into Towson relocation treatment is political pressure / a call for a probe — not an NLRB-issued complaint or ruling (pre-adjudication). Sub-threshold even if scored. Boundary watch active (0.6pt below Established).

results.name: UnitedHealth Group
results.key_finding: DOJ criminal/civil/antitrust probes are investigations, not convictions (pre-adjudication). Jun 23 DOJ takedown ($6.5B, 455 defendants) is sector context, not entity-named. Near-floor 10.2 already prices the alleged pattern. No new development Jun 27-28.

results.name: United States
results.key_finding: Baseline 17.5 set Jun 9 by the US's OWN conduct (not stale, §3e-bis(2)). DOJ Olmstead memo is policy-intent/pre-implementation; speedy-deportation resumption partly double-counts already-scored deportation conduct; 66k ICE detention is incremental. Aggregate sub-threshold. Watch flags EQU/ACC. No new development Jun 27-28.

results.name: Israel
results.key_finding: Floor reinforced: UN COI genocide finding (Jun 23) and resumed Lebanon strikes (Jun 28) are negative own-conduct. Lebanon framework did not mandate Israeli withdrawal — not positive own-conduct. No lower score exists.

results.name: Burkina Faso
results.key_finding: Near-floor reinforced: 52.8M Sahel food insecurity + second-largest IDP population continue the already-priced (Jun 1 reweighted) state-perpetration arc. Sahel calibration flag-for-review remains open at coordinator level (Mali 12.5 vs BF 6.3); no assessor one-off (§3e-bis(5)). No new development Jun 27-28.

results.name: Democratic Republic of Congo
results.key_finding: Near-floor reinforced: Ebola 1,155 cases/304 deaths (accelerating), first EU case (France Jun 24), contact tracing 45% (own-conduct response failure). M23 mass-killing harm attributed to Rwanda. Compounding already-priced crises; no new threshold. No new count Jun 27-28.

results.name: Iran
results.key_finding: Near-floor reinforced: 784+ executions YTD (37-year high), hunger strikes Week 126+ across 56 prisons. Continuation of already-priced own-conduct pattern; no new threshold.

results.name: Yemen
results.key_finding: Floor reinforced: Houthi authorities forced WFP to terminate all 365 northern staff; 73 UN staff detained, one died in custody; aid assets seized. Governing-actor own conduct. No lower score exists.

results.name: South Sudan
results.key_finding: Floor reinforced: 7.8M food insecure (56%), 2.2M children acutely malnourished, four counties at famine risk, 140,000 displaced in Akobo. No lower score exists. No major new development Jun 27-28.

results.name: Somalia
results.key_finding: Threshold-check: IPC end-of-June famine-threshold DATE passed for Buur Hakaba (AMN Phase 5, GAM 37.1%, one of three thresholds exceeded) but NO formal IPC famine declaration as of Jun 28 (verified IPC/FSNAU + FEWS NET). Compounding shock on already-priced crisis; no threshold crossing.

false_positives_screened.screened: SPECIAL-ATTENTION DETERMINATION. The Jun 24 twin earthquakes (1,430+ killed, 68,900+ missing) are a natural disaster — exogenous, not the government's conduct (Jun-6 Uganda natural-disaster-overlay-non-double-count precedent). Only the government's RESPONSE is scorable, and it is genuinely mixed: (POSITIVE) broad international aid ACCEPTED — 17 flights/1,600+ rescuers from US, Cuba, Mexico, Colombia, Brazil, El Salvador, France, UN; $200M reconstruction fund; state of emergency — which cuts against a downgrade and is the decisive differentiator from floor-cluster aid-blockers (Yemen/Houthis). (NEGATIVE) slow, disjointed, opaque relief; medical staff 'we don't know what arrived'; 14,000+ troops/permits restricting access; performative officials. Directionality (§3e-bis(3)) blocks an UPGRADE (surfaced on a negative event; aid acceptance is mild, not strong countervailing positive conduct). Double-count + threshold (§3e-bis(4)) blocks a DOWNGRADE: 18.0 already prices weak capacity and poor accountability, and a capacity-limited response — though severe — is consistent with the Critical band, not NEW deliberate harm clearing the 5-point threshold. Transparency concerns are early allegations, not documented diversion/obstruction (pre-documentation). Net sub-threshold both directions. Confirm 18.0; watch flags ACT + ACC; forward trigger = documented aid obstruction/diversion/casualty-data suppression (downgrade) or sustained effective transparent relief (upgrade).

Framework collapsed within 48 hours (Hezbollah rejection Jun 27, Israel resumed strikes Jun 28). The forward-trigger positive (implementation) did not materialize — no upgrade. Renewed war harm is Israel's conduct (scored against Israel). No new negative Lebanese own-conduct; absence of new own-harm is not positive evidence.

Mythos 5 partial unblock is US Commerce action ON Anthropic (attributed to US, not Anthropic); a partial reversal of a government restriction is neither positive nor negative Anthropic own-conduct. EU AI Act enforcement (Aug 2) is a forward trigger. Did not adopt any drifted cache; canonical 59.1.

SAF Jun 28 counterattack reopening a road is an armed-operations move, not humanitarian own-conduct; RSF's acceptance of a Quad humanitarian-ceasefire proposal is unimplemented and SAF-skeptical (forward trigger). Sudan already at the 0.0 floor; no lower score exists.

UN COI genocide finding (Jun 23) and resumed Lebanon strikes (Jun 28) are major negative own-conduct but Israel is at the 0.0 floor; no lower score exists. Framework did not mandate withdrawal — not positive own-conduct.

Buur Hakaba verified at IPC AMN Phase 5 (GAM 37.1%, one of three famine thresholds exceeded) with NO formal IPC famine declaration as of Jun 28 (IPC/FSNAU + FEWS NET). The threshold DATE passing is not the scored trigger (a formal declaration). Compounding shock on already-priced crisis; no crossing.

17.5 set Jun 9 by the US's OWN conduct — 'stale baseline' rationale invalid. DOJ Olmstead memo is policy-intent/pre-implementation; speedy-deportation resumption partly double-counts already-scored deportation conduct; detention rise incremental. Aggregate sub-threshold. Confirm + watch (EQU/ACC).

Congressional Labor Caucus call for an NLRB investigation is political pressure / a call for a probe, not an NLRB-issued complaint or ruling; sub-threshold (<5pt) even if scored. Confirm + boundary watch.
DOJ criminal/civil/antitrust probes are investigations, not convictions; Jun 23 takedown is sector context, not entity-named. Near-floor 10.2 already prices the alleged pattern.
35M Phase 3+ / 15,000 Phase 5 Borno already priced in the Jun 19 band crossing to 18.0. Re-scoring the same evidence would double-count. WFP imminent-cuts is a forward trigger.

52.8M Sahel food insecurity + IDP/siege continue the already-priced (Jun 1 reweighted) arc. The open Sahel-band calibration (Mali 12.5 vs BF 6.3) is a coordinator-level flag-for-review, not an assessor one-off.
M23 mass-killing harm attributed to Rwanda. DRC's own-conduct Ebola-response failure (45% contact tracing) reinforces the near-floor but continues already-priced compounding crises; no new threshold.

math_hygiene.warnings_note: Pre-existing warnings in us-cities.json and us-states.json; none involve assessed entities.
math_hygiene.rotation_cache_status: Stale-cache drift found and corrected: Venezuela rotation cache read 4.4 (Maduro-era) vs authoritative live-index 18.0 (drift -13.6); refreshed composite 4.4->18.0 and rank 172->151 to live-index values. All other 14 assessed-entity rotation caches matched authoritative live-index values. Baselines read from site/src/data/indexes/ (live), NOT the rotation cache, per instruction. last_assessed bumped to 2026-06-28 for all 15.

venezuela_determination: CONFIRM at 18.0 (delta 0.0, no change proposal). The earthquake is a natural disaster (not own-conduct); the government's response is the scorable conduct and is genuinely mixed — broad international aid accepted (mild positive; differentiates Venezuela from aid-blocking floor states and blocks a downgrade via §3e-bis(4) double-count/threshold) vs a slow, opaque, access-restricted relief effort (negative; blocked from yielding an upgrade by §3e-bis(3) directionality). Net sub-threshold in both directions; 18.0 already prices weak capacity and accountability. Watch flags raised on ACT (relief responsiveness) and ACC (aid-delivery transparency). Confidence medium (fast-moving event).
sahel_calibration_status: OPEN — coordinator-level Sahel-band ACTIVE-STATE-PERPETRATION-REWEIGHT review pending (Mali 12.5 vs Burkina Faso 6.3). Carried forward. Burkina Faso confirmed at 6.3 as the reweighted reference point; no assessor one-off (§3e-bis(5)).
forward_triggers_logged: Venezuela: documented deliberate aid obstruction, diversion/theft of relief, or suppression of casualty data = scorable negative own-conduct (downgrade); a demonstrably effective, transparent, sustained relief/reconstruction effort = scorable positive own-conduct (upgrade).
Lebanon: durable ceasefire + LAF sovereign control + reconstruction = positive own-conduct; expansion of internal armed conflict = negative own-conduct.
Sudan: an implemented, civilian-protecting ceasefire with unobstructed aid access = positive own-conduct.
Somalia: a formal IPC famine declaration for Buur Hakaba/Baidoa would warrant immediate re-assessment.
Nigeria: realized WFP assistance cuts and a formal Phase 5 famine declaration in Borno.
United States: formal DOJ withdrawal of Olmstead enforcement or state re-institutionalization (EQU/ACC); realized harms from resumed fast-track removals (INT/ACC).
Apple / UnitedHealth: an NLRB-issued complaint, ruling, settlement, conviction, or admitted findings would be scorable.
Anthropic: EU AI Act enforcement (Aug 2); any Congressional escalation on the Commerce export-control action is US-government conduct, not Anthropic.
